nodes.name: Band Lineups
nodes.description: Famous band members and their roles

items.prompt: Which rhythm guitarist and vocalist co-founded a Liverpool band in 1960, later writing 'Imagine' as a solo artist before being murdered in New York City in 1980?
items.explanation:
Rhythm guitarist and vocalist for The Beatles (1960–1970)
Co-wrote songs with Paul McCartney under the Lennon–McCartney partnership — the most successful songwriting duo in history
Solo career produced 'Imagine' (1971), consistently ranked among the greatest songs ever written
Married Yoko Ono in 1969; their 'Bed-Ins for Peace' became iconic anti-war protests
Murdered by Mark David Chapman outside his apartment at the Dakota, New York City, on December 8, 1980
First Beatle to release a solo album outside the group (*Unfinished Music No. 1: Two Virgins*, 1968)
Inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame twice: with The Beatles (1988) and as a solo artist (1994)

items.prompt: Which bassist and vocalist from a legendary Liverpool band went on to form Wings in 1971 and holds the Guinness record for most certified #1 hits by a songwriter?
items.explanation:
Bassist and vocalist for The Beatles (1960–1970)
Most successful songwriter in popular music history — co-wrote 20 Beatles #1 singles plus 9 more solo/Wings #1s on the Billboard Hot 100
Formed Wings with wife Linda (1971–1981); hits include 'Band on the Run' and 'Live and Let Die'
Wrote 'Yesterday' (1965), the most covered song in recorded history with 2,200+ versions
Knighted in 1997 for services to music
'Hey Jude' (1968, Lennon–McCartney) held the record for longest #1 single at 7:11
Billionaire and one of the wealthiest musicians in history

items.prompt: Which lead guitarist of a 1960s Liverpool band released 'My Sweet Lord' as a solo single and organized the Concert for Bangladesh in 1971?
items.explanation:
Lead guitarist for The Beatles (1960–1970), known as 'the quiet Beatle'
'My Sweet Lord' (1970) was the first solo #1 by a Beatle — later found to have unconsciously plagiarized 'He's So Fine' by The Chiffons
Organized the Concert for Bangladesh (1971), the first major charity concert in rock history
Introduced Indian classical music and the sitar to Western rock, studying under Ravi Shankar
*All Things Must Pass* (1970) triple album is regarded as the finest solo Beatles album
Co-founded HandMade Films, which produced Monty Python's *Life of Brian* (1979)
Died of lung cancer on November 29, 2001, at age 58

items.prompt: Which drummer replaced Pete Best in a famous Liverpool band in August 1962 and later sang lead on 'Octopus's Garden' and 'Yellow Submarine'?
items.explanation:
Drummer for The Beatles (1962–1970), born Richard Starkey in Liverpool
Replaced Pete Best on August 16, 1962, just weeks before 'Love Me Do' was recorded
Sang lead on 'Yellow Submarine,' 'With a Little Help from My Friends,' and 'Octopus's Garden' (which he wrote)
Widely praised for his inventive, left-handed drumming style despite playing a right-handed kit
Led Ringo Starr & His All-Starr Band, a rotating supergroup, from 1989 onward
Appointed Knight Bachelor in 2018 for services to music — the second Beatle knighted after McCartney
Last surviving Beatle along with McCartney

items.prompt: Which guitarist, who previously played with the Yardbirds, co-founded a hard rock band with vocalist Robert Plant in 1968 and was known for playing a Gibson EDS-1275 double-neck guitar?
items.explanation:
Guitarist and founder of Led Zeppelin (1968–1980)
One of the most prolific session guitarists of the 1960s before joining the Yardbirds in 1966
Produced all Led Zeppelin albums; pioneered layered overdubs and unconventional recording techniques
Used a Gibson EDS-1275 double-neck guitar (6-string and 12-string) for live performances of 'Stairway to Heaven'
Played with a violin bow on 'Dazed and Confused' — a signature live technique
His guitar riff on 'Whole Lotta Love' (1969) was voted the greatest guitar riff of all time by Total Guitar readers
Appointed OBE in 2005 for charitable services

items.prompt: Which vocalist fronted a 1970s hard rock band that recorded 'Stairway to Heaven' and was known for his high-range vocal style and blues-influenced singing?
items.explanation:
Lead vocalist of Led Zeppelin (1968–1980)
Vocal range and blues-influenced wail on tracks like 'Whole Lotta Love' and 'Stairway to Heaven' defined hard rock vocals
Wrote most of Led Zeppelin's lyrics, drawing on J.R.R. Tolkien, Celtic mythology, and the blues
Solo career spanned four decades; *Raising Sand* (2007) with Alison Krauss won five Grammys including Album of the Year
Turned down multiple reunion offers after Bonham's death; the 2007 O2 Arena reunion was a one-off
Appointed CBE in 2009 for services to popular music
Ranked #1 on Hit Parader's list of Greatest Heavy Metal Vocalists of All Time

items.prompt: Which Led Zeppelin member, famous for a 26-minute drum solo in 'Moby Dick,' died in 1980 from alcohol-related asphyxiation, prompting the band to disband?
items.explanation:
Drummer for Led Zeppelin (1968–1980), nicknamed 'Bonzo'
His powerful, bass-drum-heavy style on 'When the Levee Breaks' (1971) is one of the most sampled drum recordings in history
Extended drum solo 'Moby Dick' ran up to 30 minutes in live performances
Died on September 25, 1980, at age 32, from aspiration of vomit after drinking 40 measures of vodka
Led Zeppelin disbanded on December 4, 1980, stating they could not continue without him
Consistently ranked among the greatest drummers of all time by *Rolling Stone*, *Modern Drummer*, and others
Self-taught; began playing drums on pots and containers at age five

items.prompt: Which vocalist joined Fleetwood Mac in 1975 alongside guitarist Lindsey Buckingham and later had a solo hit with 'Edge of Seventeen'?
items.explanation:
Vocalist for Fleetwood Mac (1975–present), joined with Lindsey Buckingham after Mick Fleetwood heard their duo album *Buckingham Nicks*
*Rumours* (1977) sold 40+ million copies; she co-wrote 'Dreams,' the band's only U.S. #1 single
Solo career launched with *Bella Donna* (1981) — hit #1 and went quadruple platinum
'Edge of Seventeen' (1981) is her signature solo track, inspired by the death of John Lennon
Inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame twice: with Fleetwood Mac (1998) and as a solo artist (2019)
Only woman inducted twice into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
Known for her mystical stage persona, shawls, and platform boots

items.prompt: Which guitarist and vocalist joined Fleetwood Mac with Stevie Nicks in 1975, shaping the *Rumours* sound, and was fired from the band in 2018?
items.explanation:
Guitarist and vocalist for Fleetwood Mac (1975–2018)
Joined with Stevie Nicks after Mick Fleetwood heard *Buckingham Nicks* (1973); transformed the band from British blues to pop-rock
His fingerpicking guitar style and production shaped *Fleetwood Mac* (1975), *Rumours* (1977), and *Tango in the Night* (1987)
Wrote and sang 'Go Your Own Way' (1977), written about his breakup with Nicks
Fired from the band in April 2018 over conflicts with Nicks; replaced by Neil Finn and Mike Campbell
Christine McVie's death in November 2022 effectively ended the band
Began working with Mick Fleetwood again in 2025 on Fleetwood's solo material

items.prompt: Which vocalist has fronted the same rock band since 1962, was knighted in 2003, and is known for his distinctive strutting stage presence?
items.explanation:
Lead vocalist of The Rolling Stones (1962–present)
Co-writes songs with Keith Richards under the Jagger–Richards partnership; together they wrote '(I Can't Get No) Satisfaction,' 'Paint It Black,' and 'Sympathy for the Devil'
Knighted in December 2003 for services to popular music — Keith Richards reportedly called it 'a paltry honor'
One of the most recognizable frontmen in rock history; his lips-and-tongue logo (designed by John Pasche, 1971) is iconic
Fronted the Stones on tours grossing over $2 billion, making them the highest-grossing touring act of all time
Father of eight children by five women, spanning five decades
Still performing live past age 80; the Stones' *Hackney Diamonds* (2023) was their first new studio album in 18 years

items.prompt: Which Rolling Stones guitarist co-wrote '(I Can't Get No) Satisfaction' and pioneered open-G tuning in rock, playing a modified five-string Fender Telecaster?
items.explanation:
Guitarist for The Rolling Stones (1962–present), co-founder with Brian Jones and Mick Jagger
Pioneered the use of open-G tuning (removing the low E string from his Fender Telecaster, 'Micawber')
Co-wrote most Stones songs with Jagger; the riff from '(I Can't Get No) Satisfaction' (1965) came to him in a dream
Survived decades of heroin addiction; his autobiography *Life* (2010) is considered one of the greatest rock memoirs
Played rhythm and lead simultaneously using interlocking guitar parts with Ronnie Wood, a style called 'the ancient art of weaving'
Original guitarist Brian Jones drowned in his swimming pool on July 3, 1969, at age 27
*Rolling Stone* ranked him #4 on its list of the 100 Greatest Guitarists

items.prompt: Which vocalist, born in Zanzibar to a Parsi family, fronted a band known for 'Bohemian Rhapsody' and possessed a range spanning nearly four octaves?
items.explanation:
Lead vocalist and pianist for Queen (1970–1991), born Farrokh Bulsara in Stone Town, Zanzibar, in 1946
Vocal range spanned nearly four octaves (from bass low F to soprano high F); studies suggest his vibrato was unusually fast at 7.04 Hz
'Bohemian Rhapsody' (1975) fused opera, ballad, and hard rock in a six-minute single — hit #1 in the UK for nine weeks
Live Aid performance at Wembley (July 13, 1985) is widely regarded as the greatest live rock performance ever
Family fled the Zanzibar Revolution in 1964; attended boarding school in Panchgani, India
Died of AIDS-related bronchopneumonia on November 24, 1991, one day after publicly confirming his diagnosis
Designed the Queen crest logo, incorporating the zodiac signs of all four band members

items.prompt: Which Queen guitarist built his own electric guitar from a fireplace mantel as a teenager and later earned a PhD in astrophysics from Imperial College London?
items.explanation:
Guitarist for Queen (1970–present)
Built the Red Special guitar with his father Harold from an 18th-century fireplace mantel, oak table, and motorcycle valve springs — still his primary instrument
Completed a PhD in astrophysics at Imperial College London in 2007, 36 years after abandoning his thesis to focus on Queen
Thesis: 'A Survey of Radial Velocities in the Zodiacal Dust Cloud'
His layered multi-track guitar harmonies (using the Red Special and a Vox AC30 amp) defined Queen's sound
Appointed CBE in 2005 and knighted in 2023 for services to music and charity
Co-wrote 'We Will Rock You' (1977) — its stomp-stomp-clap rhythm is one of the most recognized in sports arenas worldwide

items.prompt: Which musician served as drummer for a Seattle grunge trio before founding Foo Fighters in 1994, becoming frontman and guitarist of the new band?
items.explanation:
Drummer for Nirvana (1990–1994), then founder and frontman of Foo Fighters (1994–present)
Joined Nirvana in 1990 after drumming for Scream; his powerful style drove *Nevermind* (1991) and *In Utero* (1993)
Recorded the entire first Foo Fighters album (1995) by himself, playing every instrument
Foo Fighters have won 16 Grammy Awards and sold 30+ million records worldwide
Known for marathon live performances, often exceeding three hours
Inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame twice: with Nirvana (2014) and with Foo Fighters (2021)
Only the third person inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame as a member of two different bands in the 'Performer' category
items.cardFront: Nirvana drums, then Foo Fighters frontman

items.prompt: Which vocalist and guitarist fronted a Seattle grunge band whose 1991 album *Nevermind* dethroned Michael Jackson from #1, before dying at age 27 in April 1994?
items.explanation:
Vocalist and guitarist for Nirvana (1987–1994), born in Aberdeen, Washington
*Nevermind* (1991) knocked Michael Jackson's *Dangerous* off the Billboard 200 #1 spot in January 1992
'Smells Like Teen Spirit' (1991) is widely credited with bringing grunge and alternative rock into the mainstream
Married Courtney Love (lead singer of Hole) in 1992; daughter Frances Bean Cobain born the same year
Struggled publicly with heroin addiction, chronic stomach pain, and depression
Died by suicide on April 5, 1994, at age 27, joining the '27 Club' alongside Jimi Hendrix, Janis Joplin, and Jim Morrison
His torn jeans, flannel shirts, and anti-corporate stance defined the grunge aesthetic and Generation X disaffection
